div.head: 279. Telegram From the Department of State to the Embassy in JordanSource: National Archives, RG 59, Central Files 1970–73, POL 23–9 JORDAN. Secret; Flash; Exdis. Drafted and approved by Seelye. It was repeated Flash to London.
div.p:
154431. 1. The British Ambassador has called urgently to state that his understanding of appeal which King has made to HMG is that UK
undertake airstrike against invading Syrian forces.Telegram 7568 from London, September 20, 1242Z, reported that the King contacted the British Embassy in Amman to request assistance. (Ibid., Nixon Presidential Materials, NSC Files, Box 619, Country Files, Middle East, Jordan Crisis) From your reporting we had not repeat not been aware that King had made such request of British nor is it clear that King in his appeal to us for USG assistanceReported in telegram 4951 from Amman, September 20, 0720Z. (Ibid., RG 59, Central Files 1970–73, POL 23–9 JORDAN) had specifically in mind a US airstrike.
